text: AHE (Agentic Harness Engineering) is the framework that holds the base model frozen, evolves all seven harness components automatically against rollouts, and lifts a bash-only seed from 69.7% to 77.0% in ten iterations.

evidence.quote: It holds the base model frozen, evolves all seven harness components automatically against rollouts, and lifts a bash-only seed from 69.7% to 77.0% in ten iterations.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: A coding agent's harness is everything around the model: system prompt, tool definitions, middleware, skills, sub-agents, memory.

evidence.quote: A coding agent's harness is everything around the model: system prompt, tool definitions, middleware, skills, sub-agents, memory.

arguments.surface: system prompt, tool definitions, middleware, skills, sub-agents, memory

text: Editing the system prompt as the only adaptation surface drops pass@1 by 2.3 points on Terminal-Bench 2.

evidence.quote: Editing it as the only adaptation surface drops pass@1 by 2.3 points on Terminal-Bench 2.

text: Prior automated work optimizes one component at a time, almost always the prompt or an in-context playbook (ACE, GEPA, DSPy) or the trajectory distribution (TF-GRPO, GRPO variants), leaving tools, middleware, and memory closed.

evidence.quote: Prior automated work optimizes one component at a time, almost always the prompt or an in-context playbook (ACE, GEPA, DSPy) or the trajectory distribution (TF-GRPO, GRPO variants).

text: AHE evolves the full harness as a combinatorial whole and treats every edit as a falsifiable contract verified against the next round's task outcomes.

evidence.quote: AHE evolves the full harness as a combinatorial whole and treats every edit as a falsifiable contract verified against the next round's task outcomes.

text: Component observability instantiates the harness on the NexAU framework, exposing seven editable component types as files at fixed mount points: system prompt, tool description, tool implementation, middleware, skill, sub-agent configuration, and long-term memory.

evidence.quote: The harness is instantiated on the NexAU framework, which exposes seven editable component types as files at fixed mount points: system prompt, tool description, tool implementation, middleware, skill, sub-agent configuration, and long-term memory.

text: Experience observability uses the Agent Debugger framework to distill raw rollout traces into a layered evidence corpus, with each trajectory message in its own file and per-task root-cause reports identifying failure patterns.

evidence.quote: The Agent Debugger framework distills raw rollout traces (millions of tokens) into a layered evidence corpus.

text: Decision observability requires every edit to ship with a change manifest entry naming the failure pattern it addresses, the predicted task fixes, the at-risk regressions, and the constraint level, allowing the loop to intersect predicted fixes and regressions against observed task-level deltas.

evidence.quote: Every edit ships with a change\manifest.json entry naming the failure pattern it addresses, the predicted task fixes, the at-risk regressions, and the constraint level (prompt, tool description, tool implementation, middleware, skill).

text: Ten AHE iterations on Terminal-Bench 2 lift pass@1 from 69.7% to 77.0%, outperforming human-designed harnesses (opencode 47.2%, terminus-2 62.9%, Codex-CLI 71.9%) and self-evolve baselines (ACE 68.9%, TF-GRPO 72.3%).

evidence.quote: Ten AHE iterations on Terminal-Bench 2 (89 tasks, k=2 rollouts, GPT-5.4 high reasoning, 32 hours total runtime) lift pass@1 from 69.7% to 77.0%.

text: On Hard-tier tasks, AHE slips to 53.3% behind Codex-CLI's 56.7%, traced to component interference rather than missing capability.

evidence.quote: On Hard it slips to 53.3% behind Codex-CLI's 56.7%, traced to component interference rather than missing capability.

issues.detail: 2 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: On SWE-bench-verified (500 tasks across seven repos), AHE achieves the highest aggregate at 75.6% while spending 12% fewer tokens than the seed, 21% fewer than TF-GRPO, and 32% fewer than ACE.

evidence.quote: On SWE-bench-verified (500 tasks across seven repos), AHE achieves the highest aggregate at 75.6% while spending 12% fewer tokens than the seed, 21% fewer than TF-GRPO, and 32% fewer than ACE.

issues.detail: quote spans a window `p032.s002`..`p033.s002` around the ref

text: Cross-model transfer using the same evolved workspace produces positive gains on five alternate bases: +10.1pp on deepseek-v4-flash, +6.3pp on qwen-3.6-plus, +5.1pp on gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview, and +2.3pp on both GPT-5.4 medium and xhigh.

evidence.quote: produces five positive gains: +10.1pp on deepseek-v4-flash (51.7→61.8), +6.3pp on qwen-3.6-plus (56.2→62.5), +5.1pp on gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview (36.5→41.6), and +2.3pp on both GPT-5.4 medium and xhigh.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Weaker bases benefit more from evolved harness components because they lean on the coordination patterns AHE has fixed inside tools, middleware, and memory, while stronger bases can re-derive the same coordination from the prompt cheaply.

evidence.quote: Weaker bases benefit more because they lean on the coordination patterns AHE has fixed inside tools, middleware, and memory.

text: The component ablation shows that swapping a single AHE component into the bash-only seed yields: memory alone +5.6pp, tools alone +3.3pp, middleware alone +2.2pp, and system prompt alone −2.3pp.

evidence.quote: Swapping a single AHE component into the bash-only seed: memory alone +5.6pp, tools alone +3.3pp, middleware alone +2.2pp, system prompt alone −2.3pp.

issues.detail: 2 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: The harness components ACE and TF-GRPO never edit are exactly where the gain lives.

evidence.quote: The harness components ACE and TF-GRPO never edit are exactly where the gain lives.

text: The pattern across all four case studies is that prompts say what to avoid, but execution-time enforcement is what changes outcomes, with three of the four winning fixes shipping at the tool-implementation or middleware level.

evidence.quote: The pattern across all four cases: prompts say what to avoid, but execution-time enforcement is what changes outcomes.

text: Non-additive component interaction causes three positive single-component gains summing to +11.1pp to result in only +7.3pp for full AHE, with stacking costing 3.8pp.

evidence.quote: Three positive single-component gains sum to +11.1pp, but full AHE only achieves +7.3pp.

text: Regression blindness shows that across nine evaluation rounds, the evolve agent issued 43 unique regression predictions and only 5 landed (precision 11.6%), with 40 unforeseen regressions actually occurring (recall 11.1%).

evidence.quote: Across nine evaluation rounds, the evolve agent issued 43 unique regression predictions and only 5 landed (precision 11.6%).

text: The evolve agent can justify why an edit should help but cannot reliably name what the same edit will break.

evidence.quote: The agent can justify why an edit should help.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: The authors flag that cross-benchmark and cross-model transfer evidence is encouraging, but a non-Terminal-Bench-2 evolution run is what would close the benchmark overfitting question, calling it a generalization hazard.

evidence.quote: Cross-benchmark and cross-model transfer evidence is encouraging, but a non-Terminal-Bench-2 evolution run is what would close the benchmaxxing question.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: The best recommendation is to treat AHE as a controlled research prototype that already produces a frozen harness worth studying, while waiting for evolution runs on a second benchmark before adopting the framework as a deployment-grade self-improvement loop.

evidence.quote: the best recommendation is to treat AHE as a controlled research prototype that already produces a frozen harness worth studying, while waiting for evolution runs on a second benchmark before adopting the framework as a deployment-grade self-improvement loop.

text: ML engineers can now evolve a coding agent's tools, middleware, and memory automatically against a benchmark, now that observability primitives turn each component edit into a falsifiable contract.

evidence.quote: ML engineers can now evolve a coding agent's tools, middleware, and memory automatically against a benchmark, now that observability primitives turn each component edit into a falsifiable contract.

text: The central design move is that every phase of the loop produces structured, file-level artifacts another agent can read.

evidence.quote: The central design move is that every phase of the loop produces structured, file-level artifacts another agent can read.

text: Self-justification becomes measurement.

evidence.quote: Self-justification becomes measurement.

text: The seed harness is deliberately minimal, just a single bash tool with no middleware or skills, so every component the loop adds has to earn its place against measured rollouts.

evidence.quote: The seed harness is deliberately minimal, just a single bash tool with no middleware or skills, so every component the loop adds has to earn its place against measured rollouts.
